seoTitle: How to Connect Jira to TestRail Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to configure the Jira and TestRail integration, link projects and issues, and sync bugs with test cases for better QA tracking.
intro: QA and development work best when bugs and test cases don't live in separate silos. This guide covers configuring the integration between Jira and TestRail, linking projects, and syncing issues so both teams stay aligned.

steps.heading: Review the integration overview
steps.body: Understand what the Jira and TestRail integration connects before configuring anything, since it links test management with issue tracking.

steps.heading: Configure the Jira connection
steps.body: In TestRail, set up the connection details needed to authenticate with your Jira instance.

steps.heading: Link projects and issues
steps.body: Map your TestRail projects to the corresponding Jira projects, and link individual issues to related test cases.

steps.heading: Test the integration and sync
steps.body: Run a sync to confirm bugs and test cases are updating correctly between both platforms.

faq.question: How do I connect Jira to TestRail?
faq.answer: Configure the Jira integration settings inside TestRail, authenticate the connection, then link your projects and issues.

faq.question: Can TestRail sync bugs automatically with Jira?
faq.answer: Yes, once configured, the integration can sync bugs and test case statuses between TestRail and Jira.

faq.question: Do I need admin access in both Jira and TestRail to set this up?
faq.answer: Yes, configuring the integration typically requires administrative access on both the Jira and TestRail sides.

faq.question: Why would a QA team connect Jira and TestRail?
faq.answer: Linking the two platforms keeps bug tracking and test case management aligned, improving collaboration between development and QA teams.
